hyperedges.semantics: Schema leniency, validator checks, and repair loop are coordinated to prevent hard parse crashes and enable structured repair.
Runbooks and architecture docs describe setup, execution, and staged parsing design.
Compiled AST output is validated and can feed into repair retries when shape or semantic constraints fail.

nodes.label: Batch runner: parse every problem in a JSONL file and auto-advance.  Reads premi
nodes.norm_label: batch runner: parse every problem in a jsonl file and auto-advance.  reads premi

nodes.norm_label: double negation should produce a warning (not error).

nodes.norm_label: only_if_rule kind without an implies node should fail.

nodes.norm_label: equation with missing operator/left/right should be caught.

nodes.norm_label: rule with implies-inside-implies should produce a warning suggesting meta.

nodes.label: META kind with nested implies should NOT get the nested-rule warning.
nodes.norm_label: meta kind with nested implies should not get the nested-rule warning.

nodes.label: Stage 5 Repair Loop
nodes.rationale: Repairs invalid Stage 3 AST JSON using validator issue feedback; does not solve the logic task.

nodes.label: Lenient schema + strict validator split
nodes.rationale: Invalid shapes are accepted in schema with warnings so Stage 4 validation and Stage 5 repair can correct them without crashing.

nodes.label: Frame-based AST compilation
nodes.rationale: Stage 3 compiles CNL into frame structures that are transformed into typed LogicNode AST.

nodes.label: Validator checks
nodes.rationale: Checks include unbound variables, negation consistency, node shape constraints, and solver-readiness classification.

nodes.label: Parser-only neurosymbolic pipeline
nodes.rationale: Pipeline outputs validated AST and classifications; solver stages are intentionally excluded.

nodes.label: Hugging Face Qwen/Qwen3.5-4B backend
nodes.rationale: Default backend in run docs; uses quantized Transformers path for parsing stages.

hyperedges.semantics: Schema leniency, validator checks, and repair loop are coordinated to prevent hard parse crashes and enable structured repair.
Runbooks and architecture docs describe setup, execution, and staged parsing design.
Compiled AST output is validated and can feed into repair retries when shape or semantic constraints fail.
